question: Refer to the previous exercise. The mean survival time in the IDX-treated group was 116 days $\left(S E_1=5.6ight)$. The mean survival time in the control group was $88.5\left(S E_2=1.9ight)$. Test $H_0: \mu_1=\mu_2$.
answer:
A. Hypotheses. $H_0: \mu_1=\mu_2$ versus $H_{\mathrm{a}}: \mu_1 
eq \mu_2$
B. Test statistic. $S E=\sqrt{S E_1^2+S E_2^2}=\sqrt{5.6^2+1.9^2}=5.91$ and $t_{	ext {stat }}=(116- 88.5) / 5.91=-4.65$. Since $n_1=10$ and $n_2=10, d f_{	ext {conserv }}=10-1=9$.
C. $ oldsymbol{P}$-value. The one-sided $P$-value is between 0.0005 and 0.001 (via Appendix Table C). Therefore, the two-sided $P$-value is between 0.001 and 0.002 . Using www.cytel.com/Products/StaTable/, the two-sided $P$-value $=0.0012$. The evidence against the null hypothesis is highly significant.
D. Significance level (optional). The evidence against the null hypothesis is significant at $\alpha=0.01$.
